member.name: T:Executable
member.summary: <summary> Abstract base utility class. </summary> <param name="<SdkRequestT>">the sdk request</param> <param name="<ProtoRequestT>">the proto request</param> <param name="<ResponseT>">the response</param> <param name="<O>">the O type</param>

member.name: T:Executable_2
member.summary: List of healthy and unhealthy nodes with which execution will be attempted.

member.name: P:Executable.AttemptedAllNodes
member.summary: Indicates if the request has been attempted to be sent to all nodes

member.name: P:Executable.GrpcDeadline
member.summary: When execution is attempted, a single attempt will timeout when this deadline is reached. (The SDK may subsequently retry the execution.)

member.name: T:Executable_3
member.summary: The maximum amount of time to wait between retries. Every retry attempt will increase the wait time exponentially until it reaches this time.

member.name: T:Executable_4
member.summary: The minimum amount of time to wait between retries. When retrying, the delay will start at this time and increase exponentially until it reaches the maxBackoff.

member.name: T:Executable_5
member.summary: The maximum times execution will be attempted

member.name: T:Executable_6
member.summary:
Set the account IDs of the nodes that this transaction will be submitted to.

Providing an explicit node account ID interferes with client-side load balancing of the network. By default, the SDK will pre-generate a transaction for 1/3 of the nodes on the network. If a node is down, busy, or otherwise reports a fatal error, the SDK will try again with a different node.

member.name: T:Executable_7
member.summary: Set a callback that will be called right before the request is sent. As input, the callback will receive the protobuf of the request, and the callback should return the request protobuf. This means the callback has an opportunity to read, copy, or modify the request that will be sent.

member.name: T:Executable_8
member.summary: Set a callback that will be called right before the response is returned. As input, the callback will receive the protobuf of the response, and the callback should return the response protobuf. This means the callback has an opportunity to read, copy, or modify the response that will be read.

member.name: P:Executable.Logger
member.summary: Set the Logger
member.param: the new Logger

member.name: M:Executable.GetMethod
member.summary: Called to direct the invocation of the query to the appropriate gRPC service.

member.name: M:Executable.MapResponse(TProtoResponse,AccountId,TProtoRequest)
member.summary: Called after receiving the query response from Hedera. The derived class should map into its output type.

member.name: M:Executable.GetNodeForExecute(System.Int32)
member.summary: Return the next node for execution. Will select the first node that is deemed healthy. If we cannot find such a node and have tried n nodes (n being the size of the node list), we will select the node with the smallest remaining delay. All delays MUST be executed in calling layer as this method will be called for sync + async scenarios.

member.name: M:Executable.GetExecutionState(ResponseStatus,TProtoResponse)
member.summary: Default implementation, may be overridden in subclasses (especially for query case). Called just after receiving the query response from Hedera. By default it triggers a retry when the pre-check status is {@code BUSY}.

member.name: M:Executable.Execute(Client)
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.

member.name: M:Executable.Execute(Client,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query with a timeout
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
The timeout after which the execution attempt will be cancelled.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client)
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
The timeout after which the execution attempt will be cancelled.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client,System.Action{TTransactionResponse,System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
a Action which handles the result or error.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan,System.Action{TTransactionResponse,System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
The timeout after which the execution attempt will be cancelled.
a Action which handles the result or error.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client,System.Action{TTransactionResponse},System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
a Action which consumes the result on success.
a Action which consumes the error on failure.

member.name: M:Executable.ExecuteAsync(Client,System.TimeSpan,System.Action{TTransactionResponse},System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary: Execute this transaction or query asynchronously.
member.param: The client with which this will be executed.
The timeout after which the execution attempt will be cancelled.
a Action which consumes the result on success.
a Action which consumes the error on failure.

member.name: M:Executable.LogTransaction(TransactionId,Client,Node,System.Boolean,System.Int32,TProtoResponse,System.Exception)
member.summary: Logs the transaction's parameters
member.param: the transaction's id
the client that executed the transaction
the node the transaction was sent to
whether the transaction was executed asynchronously
the attempt number
the transaction response if the transaction was successful
the error if the transaction was not successful

member.name: M:Executable.UpdateNetworkFromAddressBook(Client)
member.summary: Updates the client's network from the address book if a mirror network is configured. Logs any errors at TRACE level without throwing exceptions.
member.param: The client whose network should be updated
